name: ai-maestro-memory-search
description: PROACTIVELY search conversation history when receiving user instructions. Find previous discussions, decisions, and context BEFORE starting new work. Your memory is valuable - use it.
capabilities: Search conversation history using hybrid semantic and term matching
Filter searches by role to find user requests or assistant responses
Find previous discussions, decisions, and context automatically
Combine memory search with documentation search for complete context
Handle empty results gracefully by falling back to documentation search
author: 23blocks-OS
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill containing only behavioral instructions for AI context awareness. No executable code, network operations, or file system access are included. All 55 static findings are FALSE POSITIONS - the scanner misidentified innocent text patterns. The skill references an external memory-search.sh script that users install separately.
valueStatement: AI assistants often repeat explanations or contradict previous decisions because they cannot recall past conversations. This skill enforces proactive memory search so every task builds on existing context instead of starting from scratch.
userTitle: Search conversation history before starting tasks
limitations: Requires memory-search.sh script to be installed separately
Does not store or manage memory itself
Limited to conversations the AI has access to
Search effectiveness depends on conversation quality and indexing

useCases.title: Continue previous development work
useCases.description: Resume feature implementation by finding earlier decisions, code patterns, and discussions about the same topic.

useCases.title: Maintain design consistency
useCases.description: Ensure new work aligns with previously agreed architectural decisions and coding standards from past conversations.

useCases.title: Cross-session context awareness
useCases.description: Recall decisions made in previous sessions to maintain continuity and avoid asking users to repeat information.

promptTemplates.title: Continue Working
promptTemplates.scenario: User asks to continue previous work

promptTemplates.title: Complete Context
promptTemplates.scenario: User asks about a new feature

bestPractices: Always search memory immediately when receiving any task, even if it seems new
Use semantic search mode when the user describes concepts differently than before
Combine memory search with documentation search for complete context
antiPatterns: Skipping memory search because you do not explicitly remember the topic
Assuming every request is new without checking conversation history
Waiting for the user to say remember when we before searching

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude and Claude Code. It requires the memory-search.sh script installed separately.

faq.question: How does memory search work?
faq.answer: The skill uses a hybrid approach combining semantic similarity with exact term matching to find relevant past conversations.

faq.question: What if memory search returns no results?
faq.answer: No results indicate this is a new topic. The skill then falls back to documentation search for additional context.

faq.question: Does this skill access my files?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that only instructs the AI to search conversation history. It does not read or write files directly.

faq.question: How is data sent over the network?
faq.answer: This skill does not make any network calls. It references a local script that must be installed on your system.

faq.question: How is this different from regular context?
faq.answer: Regular context only includes the current conversation. Memory search accesses past conversations across sessions to prevent repetition.
